[Stadio Comunale Romeo Menti](/wiki/Stadio_Romeo_Menti_(Castellammare_di_Stabia) "Stadio Romeo Menti (Castellammare di Stabia)")

is an Italian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [club](/wiki/List_of_football_clubs_in_Italy "List of football clubs in Italy") based in [Castellammare di Stabia](/wiki/Castellammare_di_Stabia "Castellammare di Stabia"), [Campania](/wiki/Campania "Campania").

and since the summer 2003 with the current name. At the end of the season, it was promoted to [Serie C2](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione "Lega Pro Seconda Divisione") and in the next year, to [Serie C1](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione"). In the 2008–09 season, the club was relegated to [Lega Pro Seconda Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione "Lega Pro Seconda Divisione"), but was immediately promoted the next season.

**Società Sportiva Juve Stabia** is an Italian professional [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [club](/wiki/List_of_football_clubs_in_Italy "List of football clubs in Italy") based in [Castellammare di Stabia](/wiki/Castellammare_di_Stabia "Castellammare di Stabia"), [Campania](/wiki/Campania "Campania").

Juve Stabia plays in [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B"), the second tier of [Italian football system](/wiki/Italian_football_system "Italian football system"), following their promotion ahead of the 2024–25 season.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SS_Juve_Stabia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### The football in Castellammare di Stabia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SS_Juve_Stabia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: The football in Castellammare di Stabia")]

#### From Stabia S.C. to S.S. Juventus Stabia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SS_Juve_Stabia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: From Stabia S.C. to S.S. Juventus Stabia")]

Main article: [History of S.S. Juve Stabia](/wiki/History_of_S.S._Juve_Stabia "History of S.S. Juve Stabia")

##### Stabia S.C.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SS_Juve_Stabia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Stabia S.C.")]

The origins of football in the town of [Castellammare di Stabia](/wiki/Castellammare_di_Stabia "Castellammare di Stabia") in the [Metropolitan City of Naples](/wiki/Metropolitan_City_of_Naples "Metropolitan City of Naples") can be traced to 19 March 1907,[[1]](#cite_note-1) when Weiss, the Romano brothers, and Pauzano founded the club as **Stabia Sporting Club**.[[2]](#cite_note-CalcioDiEccellenza.it-2) In 1930, the club changed its name to **F.C. Stabiese**, and in summer 1933, it filed for bankruptcy.

##### A.C. Stabia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SS_Juve_Stabia&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: A.C. Stabia")]

Stabia was refounded as **A.C. Stabia** in 1933.

During the [1951–52 season](/wiki/1951%E2%80%9352_Serie_B "1951–52 Serie B"), it played in [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B").

In 1953, it was declared bankrupt.

##### S.S. Juventus Stabia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SS_Juve_Stabia&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: S.S. Juventus Stabia")]

In 1953, the second club of the town, **Società Sportiva Juventus Stabia**, founded in 1945, became the main team of [Castellammare di Stabia](/wiki/Castellammare_di_Stabia "Castellammare di Stabia") and inherited the sporting tradition of the former club.

In 2001, the club declared bankruptcy.

#### From Comprensorio Stabia to S.S. Juve Stabia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SS_Juve_Stabia&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: From Comprensorio Stabia to S.S. Juve Stabia")]

In the summer of 2002, entrepreneur Paolo D'Arco acquired the sports rights of [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") of [Comprensorio Nola](/wiki/History_of_S.S._Nola#From_Virtus_Nola_1925_to_Comprensorio_Nola "History of S.S. Nola") and immediately renamed it **Comprensorio Stabia** and since the summer 2003 with the current name. At the end of the season, it was promoted to [Serie C2](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione "Lega Pro Seconda Divisione") and in the next year, to [Serie C1](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione"). In the 2008–09 season, the club was relegated to [Lega Pro Seconda Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Seconda_Divisione "Lega Pro Seconda Divisione"), but was immediately promoted the next season.

In the 2010–11 season, Juve Stabia was promoted in Serie B after 59 years. It played in the Italian second division for three consecutive seasons before being relegated in 2013–14. It returned to the second tier for the 2019–20 season (getting relegated after one season), and then again for the 2024–25 season.
